nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Modulus of elasticity measures stiffness of materials
nodes.description: The modulus of elasticity, also known as Young's modulus, is a measure of the stiffness or resistance to deformation of a material, defined as the ratio of stress to strain within the proportional limit of the material.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Higher modulus of elasticity indicates stiffer materials
nodes.description: A higher modulus of elasticity indicates that the material is stiffer and less likely to deform under load, while a lower modulus indicates that the material is more flexible and more likely to deform.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Modulus of elasticity is important in engineering and materials science
nodes.description: The modulus of elasticity is an important material property in engineering and materials science, used to design and analyze structures and components subjected to external forces.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Moment of inertia is a geometric property of cross-sectional shapes
nodes.description: The moment of inertia, also known as the second moment of area, describes a cross-sectional shape's resistance to bending or torsion.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Moment of inertia formula varies by cross-sectional shape
nodes.description: The formula for the moment of inertia I depends on the specific cross-sectional shape of the object, with examples including rectangular, circular, hollow circular, and triangular cross-sections.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Stress-strain diagram represents relationship between stress and strain
nodes.description: A stress-strain diagram is a graphical representation of the relationship between stress and strain in a material, with stress on the y-axis and strain on the x-axis.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Stress-strain diagram has three regions: elastic, plastic, and failure
nodes.description: The stress-strain diagram is divided into three regions: 1. Elastic region, where the material behaves elastically; 2. Plastic region, where the material deforms plastically; 3. Failure region, where the material fails.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Percent elongation measures material ductility
nodes.description: The percent elongation is a measure of a material's ductility, defined as the increase in length of a specimen after a tensile test, expressed as a percentage of the original length.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Poisson's ratio measures lateral strain to axial strain
nodes.description: Poisson's ratio is defined as the negative ratio of the transverse strain to the axial strain, describing how a material deforms laterally when stretched or compressed axially.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Poisson's ratio can be found through tensile testing
nodes.description: One method to find Poisson's ratio is to perform a tensile test, measuring longitudinal and lateral strains to calculate the ratio.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Poisson's ratio can also be found using flexural testing
nodes.description: Another method to find Poisson's ratio is through flexural testing, where a beam-shaped specimen is loaded and deflection is measured to calculate strains.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 17:29] Poisson's ratio can be determined using ultrasonic testing
nodes.description: Poisson's ratio can also be found using ultrasonic testing methods, where the velocities of longitudinal and transverse waves are measured.

nodes.description: The Basel tram system includes 13 different tram lines connecting the city and suburbs, known for their efficiency and punctuality since their introduction in 1895.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Basel has a comprehensive bus network connecting neighborhoods and suburbs
nodes.description: In addition to trams, Basel's public transportation includes a bus network that connects different neighborhoods and suburbs, along with regional trains connecting to other parts of Switzerland and neighboring countries.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Basel's public transportation prices include CHF 3.60 for a single trip and CHF 9.00 for a day pass
nodes.description: In Basel, a single trip within city limits on a tram or bus costs CHF 3.60 for adults and CHF 2.00 for children, while a day pass for unlimited travel costs CHF 9.00 for adults and CHF 4.50 for children.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Basel's public transportation prices are generally similar to Zurich
nodes.description: A single trip within Zurich costs CHF 4.30 for adults and CHF 2.30 for children, while a day pass for unlimited travel costs CHF 9.80 for adults and CHF 5.00 for children, making Basel's prices comparable.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Basel's public transportation prices are lower compared to Geneva

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Public transportation in Basel can get busy during peak hours

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Tickets for public transportation in Basel can be purchased in several ways
nodes.description: In Basel, tickets can be bought at ticket machines, ticket offices, or online, with many machines accepting both cash and credit cards.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03 02:29] Online ticket purchases for Basel's public transportation are available through BVB
